specific_location: South Shore Marina, Pueblo Lake State Park
description: This is still a rare bird in CO. Just to document its prescence at Lake Pueblo State Park for another winter.  Huge dark mantled gull. Undersides gleaming white. Head white with a few black streaks. Neck white with a few black streaks. Flat crown. Heavy bill with reddish gonys spot. Red orbital ring. Dark eye. Legs and feet pinkish. Mantle gray black with wings slightly more black. Trail edge to wings broadly white. Extensive white wing tips on p9 and p10.  StillBehaviors: Seen mostly loafing bathing.Call: nonePlumage: adult basic
